series_metadata.series_description: When a school district educates a special needs student who is not a resident of the district, that district may charge the district of residence “excess costs”. Excess costs are defined as the cost to educate the student minus the amount of State and local funds received for educating the student. Districts incurring excess costs may seek reimbursement after the end of the fiscal year by filing an excess cost application and providing supporting documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: 5 years, provided audits by the Auditor of State have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved. ; Electronic: 5 years, provided audits by the Auditor of State have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved., Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Delete
